ELPAC Level 4
Lexile ≥1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Seventy Years Early

STS-MS7-L4-1400P-MSSS7-FFR-API-02 • MS Social Studies Grade 7 • The Reformation
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
At the Forecast Bureau, every reporter's job is to look at one invention and predict what it will change. Metal movable type was cast in Korea and used to print the Jikji in 1377, about seventy years before a press was built in Europe. Once ideas could be printed fast and cheap, a single posted list of complaints in 1517 spread across a continent in weeks instead of years. The reporters here are printing their forecasts on the same kind of machine they are writing about.
If you were a reporter in 1517, what would you predict would change first once anyone could print their ideas quickly?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Minji doing?
Look at the picture. Build a case for what Minji is doing, cite the visible evidence, and infer what her role tells you about how this group shares its findings. Use precise academic language.
2
What is Haruto holding?
Look at the picture. Build a case for what Haruto is holding, cite the visible evidence, and infer why a group like this would display its work rather than store it. Use precise academic language.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Build a case for what the teacher is doing, cite the visible evidence, and infer why this scene puts an adult on that particular step. Use precise academic language.
4
What place is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Build a case for what kind of place this is, cite at least three pieces of evidence, and judge which is strongest and which could mislead a viewer. Use precise academic language.
5
Tell me about the whole forecast board.
Look at the picture. Build a case for what the tall board is asking, cite what you can read, and infer what the people here are being asked to predict. Use precise academic language.
6
What is Haruto wearing?
Look at the picture. Build a case for how appearance functions as a signal in this scene, using Haruto as your example, and infer why the designer of the scene made those choices. Use precise academic language.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: HSS 7.9 — The Reformation. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
